categories.summary:
# Analysis of Discord Chat in 💬｜general Channel

## 1. Summary
The chat primarily revolves around users trying to install and use Eliza v2 beta, which is currently experiencing various installation and setup issues. Users are struggling with the installation process, particularly with dependencies like bun, and many are unable to access default agents like Eliza. Shaw and jin provide troubleshooting assistance throughout the conversation, acknowledging that the beta version still has issues to be fixed. There are discussions about LLM models suitable for Eliza, with chris.troutner mentioning that deepseek-r1:14b works well with the Eliza framework due to its ability to follow instructions, despite not being the fastest model. The development team is actively collecting feedback on bugs and issues to prioritize fixes for the v2 release.

## 2. FAQ
Q: Should we start using v2 or should we wait until merged to main? Is it stable enough to use in production? (asked by 0xn1c0) A: I don't think it's stable enough to be used in production for a business. It's officially in beta. (answered by chris.troutner)
Q: How to install eliza? (asked by Beowoolf) A: Eliza.how (answered by jin)
Q: What all chains can Eliza have create agent wallets on? (asked by Scooter) A: Yes any evm, couple dozen chains supported (answered by jin)
Q: Is it fast? (asked by volenn) A: Yes. I'm getting around 35 tokens per second on a GeForce 3060 with 12GB of VRAM on the deepseek-r1:14b model. (answered by chris.troutner)
Q: How do I import those agents into my terminal? (asked by 古奇 黑鬼) A: git clone https://github.com/elizaos/eliza && cd eliza && bun install && bun run start (answered by shaw)
Q: What is the latest stable version and how to install it? (asked by Tiki) A: v2-develop branch (answered by yung_algorithm)

## 3. Help Interactions
Helper: shaw | Helpee: m4ss | Context: User struggling to install and run Eliza with agents | Resolution: Provided installation commands and troubleshooting steps, identified that bun needs to be installed first
Helper: shaw | Helpee: tuck | Context: User unable to access Eliza agents in the new beta | Resolution: Identified that the default agent creation wasn't working properly, promised to fix it in the next day or two
Helper: jin | Helpee: 4n7m4n | Context: User trying to run incorrect command (npx eliza start) | Resolution: Clarified that the correct command is npx elizaos, not eliza, and provided documentation links
Helper: shaw | Helpee: 古奇 黑鬼 | Context: User unable to see agents after installation | Resolution: Provided git clone command but acknowledged there's an issue with default agent creation
Helper: 0xbbjoker | Helpee: jin | Context: User encountering database migration error | Resolution: Explained the issue is due to schema changes and suggested wiping and starting again

## 4. Action Items
Type: Technical | Description: Fix default agent creation in beta version | Mentioned By: shaw
Type: Technical | Description: Fix startup issues documented in GitHub issue #3989 | Mentioned By: jin
Type: Technical | Description: Fix broken link on elizaos.ai/framework that 404s to .gg | Mentioned By: cjft
Type: Technical | Description: Address database migration errors with relation "agents" already exists | Mentioned By: jin
Type: Technical | Description: Fix chunk size in knowledge table for better text similar search | Mentioned By: Sabochee
Type: Documentation | Description: Map out all areas of getting started and verify steps work | Mentioned By: jin
Type: Documentation | Description: Create better documentation for non-developers to install and use Eliza | Mentioned By: shaw
Type: Feature | Description: Create an .exe version for easier installation | Mentioned By: shaw
Type: Technical | Description: Fix RAG search error with MongoDB | Mentioned By: y`Sir bLack
Type: Documentation | Description: Document migration process from generic embeddings to OpenAI with RAG | Mentioned By: soyrubio

# Analysis of "🎤｜plug-your-projects" Channel

## 1. Summary
The chat segment is very brief, containing only two messages. NaN announced a project called aigent.sh, which aims to make AI Agents accessible to non-technical users. The platform is built using ElizaOS technology. NaN mentioned that they are releasing daily updates and requested feedback, offering prioritized early access to those who send a direct message. Odilitime responded with a brief question about whether the project had been submitted to "the registry," presumably referring to some form of project registry relevant to the community.

## 2. FAQ
Q: Did you submit it to the registry? (asked by Odilitime) A: Unanswered

## 3. Help Interactions
No significant help interactions were present in this brief chat segment.

## 4. Action Items
Feature: Submit aigent.sh to the registry | Description: Consider registering the project in the community registry | Mentioned By: Odilitime

# Discord Chat Analysis for 🤖｜agent-dev-school

## 1. Summary
The chat segment contains minimal technical discussion. Users are inquiring about upcoming dev-school streams, promotional materials, and experiencing technical issues. Two specific technical problems were mentioned: one user having trouble with the CoinGecko plugin, and another encountering a "better-sqlite3" error when trying to start a Twitter agent. There was also a brief exchange about an out-of-memory error, with a suggestion to check RAM availability. Overall, this segment represents users seeking help rather than in-depth technical discussions or problem-solving.

## 2. FAQ
Q: When are the next dev-school streams? (asked by KaiserMerkle) A: Unanswered
Q: Can someone help me out with the congecko plugin? (asked by La flame) A: Unanswered
Q: How much ram do you have? (asked by Odilitime) A: Unanswered

## 3. Help Interactions
Helper: Odilitime | Helpee: Unknown | Context: Out of memory error | Resolution: Suggested checking RAM availability

## 4. Action Items
Technical: Resolve CoinGecko plugin issues | Description: User experiencing problems with the plugin | Mentioned By: La flame
Technical: Fix better-sqlite3 error | Description: Error when starting Twitter agent with "pnpm start" | Mentioned By: Chen0xA3
Documentation: Create promotional material | Description: Something to promote the next stream | Mentioned By: jin
Technical: Review unspecified pull request | Description: User requested review of a pull request | Mentioned By: fforbeck

# Analysis of Discord Chat in 📮｜feedback Channel

## 1. Summary
The discussion centers on a technical issue encountered while building a crypto wallet with an Eliza agent. ShisukeUrahara describes a problem where the agent fails to recognize when a wallet has been funded after previously determining there were insufficient funds. Specifically, when the user requests to transfer ETH and the agent determines there are insufficient funds, the agent continues to reference this outdated information even after the wallet has been funded. The only workaround found was to delete the memories table from the database, forcing the agent to check the current balance instead of relying on conversation history. This highlights a significant limitation in how the agent handles state changes that occur outside its direct observation (like on-chain transactions) and suggests a need for improved context management for blockchain applications.

## 2. FAQ
Q: Is there any better way to deal with the issue of agents not recognizing external state changes like wallet funding? (asked by ShisukeUrahara) A: Unanswered

## 3. Help Interactions
No help interactions were present in this chat segment.

## 4. Action Items
Type: Technical | Description: Implement a fix for agents to recognize external state changes (like on-chain transactions) without requiring memory deletion | Mentioned By: ShisukeUrahara
Type: Feature | Description: Add capability for agents to verify current blockchain state rather than relying solely on conversation history | Mentioned By: ShisukeUrahara
